exercises.title: Trace static counter
exercises.scenario: Static field shared by all instances.
hints: count is static — one shared value
Each new Widget() calls the constructor → count++
Three Widgets created → count = 3
getCount() returns the static field

exercises.title: Declare a constant
exercises.scenario: Practice the static final constant pattern.
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blanks to declare a constant named MAX_USERS with value 1000.
hints: Constants use: public static final
static = one copy, final = immutable
Name in SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE

exercises.title: Simple static utility method
exercises.instructions:
Create a class `StringUtils` with a STATIC method `countVowels(String s)` that counts vowels (a, e, i, o, u — case-insensitive).

Call it without creating any StringUtils object.

Expected output:
Vowels in 'Hello World': 3
exercises.starter_code: // Write StringUtils class with static countVowels method
hints: Method is static — callable on class name
No instance of StringUtils needed
Call: StringUtils.countVowels(...)
Works purely on its parameter — no instance state needed

exercises.title: Fix the static context error
exercises.scenario: Cannot access instance field from static context.
exercises.instructions:
This code doesn't compile. Fix it.

Expected output:
5

bugs.issue: Static method `doubled()` tries to access instance field `value`. There's no 'current object' in a static context.
bugs.fix: Remove 'static' from doubled(). Also add a getter since value is private.

hints: Compilation error: non-static variable value cannot be referenced from a static context
doubled() needs THIS object's value — it should be an instance method
Remove 'static' from doubled()
value is private — add a getter or use the doubled() method

exercises.title: Instance counter class
exercises.scenario: Classic static counter tracking how many instances exist.
exercises.instructions:
Create a `User` class where each new user gets an auto-assigned id:
- Private static field: nextId = 1
- Private field: id (int)
- Private field: name (String)
- Constructor: takes only name. Sets this.id = nextId++
- Static method getNextId() shows next id to be assigned
- toString: 'User[id=X, name=Y]'

In main, create 3 users and print each.

Expected output:
User[id=1, name=Aisha]
User[id=2, name=Raj]
User[id=3, name=Priya]
exercises.starter_code:
// Build User class with auto-id assignment
// nextId is static — shared across all users

test_cases.expected_stdout:
User[id=1, name=Aisha]
User[id=2, name=Raj]
User[id=3, name=Priya]

hints: nextId is static — one value shared across all users
Constructor: this.id = nextId++ (uses current value, then increments)
Post-increment: nextId++ returns current value, then increments
First user gets id=1, second gets 2, etc.

exercises.title: Constants class
exercises.scenario: Define application-level constants in a utility class.
exercises.instructions:
Create a `HttpStatus` class with ONLY static final constants (no instance state):
- `OK` = 200
- `CREATED` = 201
- `NOT_FOUND` = 404
- `SERVER_ERROR` = 500
- Private constructor (prevent instantiation)

In main, print the constant values.

Expected output:
OK: 200
NOT_FOUND: 404
exercises.starter_code:
// Build HttpStatus class with constants
// Private constructor prevents instantiation
hints: Each constant: public static final int NAME = value
SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE for names
Private constructor: private HttpStatus() {}
Access via class name: HttpStatus.OK

exercises.title: Factory method pattern
exercises.scenario: Static factory methods with semantic names.
exercises.instructions:
Create a `Temperature` class:
- Private final field: celsius (double)
- PRIVATE constructor (prevents direct `new Temperature(x)`)
- Static factory: `fromCelsius(double c)` — creates from Celsius
- Static factory: `fromFahrenheit(double f)` — converts F→C, creates  (formula: (f-32)/1.8)
- Static factory: `fromKelvin(double k)` — converts K→C, creates (formula: k-273.15)
- Getter: getCelsius()
- toString: '<value>°C'

In main:
- Create 3 temperatures using different factories
- Print each

Expected output:
100.0°C
100.0°C
100.0°C
exercises.starter_code:
// Build Temperature class with factory methods
// Private constructor — only factories can create instances
hints: Private constructor: private Temperature(double celsius)
Factory methods are public static, return `new Temperature(...)`
Semantic naming: callers say 'I have Fahrenheit' and use fromFahrenheit

exercises.title: Static utility class
exercises.scenario: Build a utility class with only static methods and constants.
exercises.instructions:
Create a `MathExtra` utility class:
- Constants: PI = 3.14159265358979, GOLDEN_RATIO = 1.61803398874989
- Private constructor
- Static method `circleArea(double r)` → π × r²
- Static method `fibonacci(int n)` → nth Fibonacci number (1-indexed: fib(1)=1, fib(2)=1, fib(3)=2)

In main:
- Print circle area for r=5
- Print fib(7)

Expected output:
Circle area (r=5): 78.539816...
Fib(7): 13
exercises.starter_code:
// Build MathExtra utility class
// Private constructor, only static methods and constants
hints: All methods static, no instance fields
Private constructor prevents instantiation
circleArea uses PI constant
fibonacci iterative: start with a=1, b=1, loop n-1 times

exercises.title: Fix the non-constant constant
exercises.instructions:
This code compiles but has a design flaw — someone can change 'MAX_SIZE'. Fix it and prevent modification.

Expected output:
100
100

bugs.issue: Missing `final` — MAX_SIZE can be reassigned by anyone
bugs.fix: Add final: `public static final int MAX_SIZE = 100;`. Remove the reassignment line (it would be a compile error).

hints: static alone doesn't prevent modification
Need final to make it immutable
public static final = true constant
With final, the line Config.MAX_SIZE = 999 is a compile error — remove it

exercises.title: Singleton pattern
exercises.instructions:
Implement a Singleton `AppConfig` class:
- Private static field: instance (AppConfig)
- Private field: String dbUrl
- Private constructor sets a default dbUrl
- Static `getInstance()` returns the single instance (create on first call)
- Getter for dbUrl, setter for dbUrl

In main:
- Get instance twice
- Verify they're the same object (==)
- Set dbUrl via first reference
- Get dbUrl via second reference (should reflect the change — same object)

Expected output:
Same instance: true
Updated URL via ref1: jdbc:mysql://prod
URL via ref2: jdbc:mysql://prod
exercises.starter_code:
// Implement Singleton AppConfig
// Private static instance, private constructor, static getInstance()

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Same instance: true
Updated URL via ref1: jdbc:mysql://prod
URL via ref2: jdbc:mysql://prod

hints: private static AppConfig instance; (starts as null)
private constructor prevents direct instantiation
getInstance: if null, create new AppConfig; then return instance
Both ref1 and ref2 point to the same instance → == is true
Change via ref1 affects ref2 (same object)

exercises.title: Static factory with caching
exercises.scenario: Factory that reuses objects instead of always creating new ones.
exercises.instructions:
Create a `Boolean2` class (simplified Boolean):
- Two static final instances: TRUE = new Boolean2(true), FALSE = new Boolean2(false)
- Private final field: value (boolean)
- Private constructor
- Static factory `of(boolean b)` — returns TRUE if b is true, FALSE if false (NO new object created)
- Getter isTrue()

In main:
- Boolean2 a = Boolean2.of(true)
- Boolean2 b = Boolean2.of(true)
- Print: a == b (should be TRUE — same cached object)

Expected output:
Same instance: true
exercises.starter_code:
// Boolean2 with static cached instances
// of() returns existing instances, not new ones
hints: Two static final instances: TRUE and FALSE
Private constructor — nobody else can create instances
of(b) returns TRUE or FALSE (the static ones) — no new object
a == b is true because both are the same static TRUE instance
This is how java.lang.Boolean works internally

exercises.title: Static vs instance interaction
hints: total is static — accumulates across all Containers
items is instance — each has its own
c1.getItems() = 5, c2.getItems() = 8
Container.getTotal() = 5 + 8 + 3 = 16

exercises.title: ID generator utility
exercises.instructions:
Create an `IdGenerator` class:
- Private static field: counters for different TYPES (use simple int[] with type-specific slots)
- Actually simpler: use separate static counters for 'USER' and 'ORDER'
- Static method `nextUserId()` → returns next user ID (1, 2, 3...)
- Static method `nextOrderId()` → returns next order ID (1, 2, 3...)
- Both start at 1

Use this to create 3 User IDs and 2 Order IDs, print them.

Expected output:
User IDs: 1 2 3
Order IDs: 1 2
exercises.starter_code: // IdGenerator with separate static counters for different entity types
hints: Separate static counters: userCount, orderCount
Pre-increment in return: return ++userCount (increments first, returns new value)
This gives 1, 2, 3 instead of 0, 1, 2
Private constructor: utility class isn't instantiated
User and order counters are independent

exercises.title: Conversion utility with constants
exercises.instructions:
Create a `UnitConverter` class:
- Constants: KG_TO_LB = 2.20462, KM_TO_MILES = 0.621371, C_TO_F_MULT = 1.8, C_TO_F_ADD = 32
- Static methods: kgToLbs(double kg), kmToMiles(double km), celsiusToFahrenheit(double c)
- Each returns the converted value

In main:
- 70 kg to lbs
- 5 km to miles
- 100°C to F (should be 212)

Expected output:
70 kg = 154.3234 lbs
5 km = 3.10686 miles
100°C = 212.0°F
exercises.starter_code: // Build UnitConverter with constants and static conversion methods
hints: Constants use public static final
Methods use constants internally
Private constructor — utility class

exercises.title: Registry class
exercises.scenario: Static registry pattern — class-level catalog of instances.
exercises.instructions:
Create a `ServiceRegistry` system:
1. `Service` class: private final String name, private final String version. Constructor + getters + toString.

2. `ServiceRegistry` class:
   - Private static `Service[]` registered (max 10)
   - Private static int count
   - Private constructor
   - Static `register(Service s)` — adds to array
   - Static `findByName(String name)` — returns Service or null
   - Static `printAll()` — prints all registered services
   - Static `getCount()` — returns count

In main:
- Register 3 services (Auth 1.0, Payment 2.1, Notification 1.5)
- Print count
- Find and print 'Payment'
- printAll()

Expected output:
Registered services: 3
Found: Service[name=Payment, version=2.1]
All services:
Service[name=Auth, version=1.0]
Service[name=Payment, version=2.1]
Service[name=Notification, version=1.5]
exercises.starter_code:
// Build Service and ServiceRegistry classes
// Registry uses static arrays — class-level catalog

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Registered services: 3
Found: Service[name=Payment, version=2.1]
All services:
Service[name=Auth, version=1.0]
Service[name=Payment, version=2.1]
Service[name=Notification, version=1.5]

hints: ServiceRegistry has NO instance — only static state and methods
Private static Service[] registered = new Service[10]
Private static int count tracks how many are filled
register: registered[count++] = s
findByName: linear search using getName().equals(name)
printAll: loop 0 to count-1, print each
